name: dnstracer
title: dnstracer
summary: trace DNS queries to the source
description:
trace DNS queries to the source
dnstracer determines where a given Domain Name Server (DNS) gets its
information from for a given hostname, and follows the chain of DNS
servers back to the authoritative answer.

commands.name: dnstracer
commands.description: trace a chain of DNS servers to the source

arguments.description: Enable overview of received answers at the end.
arguments.raw: -o  Enable overview of received answers at the end.

arguments.description: Be verbose on what sent or received.
arguments.raw: -v  Be verbose on what sent or received.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use dnstracer because trace DNS queries to the source.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use dnstracer as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnstracer [options] name` as documented upstream for dnstracer.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnstracer www.mavetju.org` as documented upstream for dnstracer.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnstracer "-s" . "-q" mx mavetju.org` as documented upstream for dnstracer.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `dnstracer "-q" ptr $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.in-addr.arpa` as documented upstream for dnstracer.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair dnstracer with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `dnstracer --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

arguments.name: -o
arguments.description: Enable overview of received answers at the end.

arguments.name: -v
arguments.description: Be verbose on what sent or received.

documentation.readme: dnstracer - trace a chain of DNS servers to the source
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install dnstracer. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `dnstracer www.mavetju.org`. Further invocations from upstream: `dnstracer "-s" . "-q" mx mavetju.org`; `dnstracer "-q" ptr $KFIOSA_TARGET_HOST.in-addr.arpa`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: trace DNS queries to the source.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: dnstracer - trace a chain of DNS servers to the source.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install dnstracer
Install with: sudo apt install dnstracer
